title: Criminal damage as defined in Criminal Damage Act 1971
summary: 9084 Criminal — destroying or damaging property belonging to another without lawful excuse.
learningObjectives: Define criminal damage under s1 CDA 1971.
Apply aggravated and arson offences under s1(2)–(3).

simpleExplanation.title: Damaging property
simpleExplanation.summary: Criminal damage is intentionally or recklessly destroying or damaging another's property — lawful excuse covers protection of property and consent.
steps: Destroy or damage — not need be permanent.
Property — s10, includes land and chattels.
Belonging to another — includes tenant's fixtures.
Lawful excuse — s5, belief in consent or protection.
